root_cause.text: The API endpoint `api/users/search` improperly included the `externalIdentity` field in the response for all authenticated users without checking if the user had system administrator privileges.
root_cause.evidence: The patch moves the code `setIfNeeded(FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY, ...)` to a block restricted to system administrators, and adds the test `return_external_identity_only_when_system_administer` to verify this behavior.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The API endpoint `api/users/search` improperly included the `externalIdentity` field in the response for all authenticated users without checking if the user had system administrator privileges.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch moves the code `setIfNeeded(FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY, ...)` to a block restricted to system administrators, and adds the test `return_external_identity_only_when_system_administer` to verify this behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update API documentation changelog for external identity visibility.
intent_record.what: Added a changelog entry to the API definition.
intent_record.how: Updates the API documentation metadata to inform users of a security-related response change.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove external identity from the user search response.
intent_record.what: Removed the line that sets the external identity field on the user builder.
intent_record.how: Prevents the user's external login identity from being included in the action's output or response.

intent_record.intent_summary: Include external identity in user search results when requested.
intent_record.what: Added a setIfNeeded call for FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY.
intent_record.how: Populates the external identity in the user response builder if requested.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify that external user identity information is only exposed to system administrators in the search endpoint.
intent_record.what: Added two new test methods: `return_external_information` and `return_external_identity_only_when_system_administer` to the `SearchActionTest` class.
intent_record.how: The new tests verify that external identity and provider information are correctly included in the search response when the user is a system administrator, and excluded otherwise.

grouping_result.intent: Remove external identity from the user search response.
Include external identity in user search results when requested.
Add tests to verify that external user identity information is only exposed to system administrators in the search endpoint.

consistency_review.core_intent: Remove external identity from the user search response and update the API documentation accordingly.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by removing the improperly exposed 'externalIdentity' field from the user search response and updating the corresponding API documentation.
The group modifies the inclusion of the 'externalIdentity' field in user search results, which is directly related to the vulnerability's root cause involving the improper exposure of this field.
The group adds tests specifically to verify the fix for the vulnerability, ensuring that external identity information is only exposed to system administrators.

candidates.rationale: This line improperly includes the external identity in the response without checking if the user has system administrator privileges.
This line improperly includes the external provider in the response without checking for admin privileges, related to external identity.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement improperly includes the external identity field in the response for all authenticated users without checking if the user has system administrator privileges.
This statement improperly includes the external provider field in the response, related to the external identity vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a124513d1f1ec3940f39e326802c4ad0c5cf1ddc
Author: Julien Lancelot <julien.lancelot@sonarsource.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 23 16:41:34 2018 +0200

    SONAR-10599 Synchronize login during authentication

diff --git a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java b/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
index b869f805f22..961b4825650 100644
--- a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
+++ b/s
bic_trace_step.reason: The function unconditionally includes the external identity field via `setIfNeeded(FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY, fields, user.getExternalLogin(), userBuilder::setExternalIdentity);` without checking for system administrator privileges, meaning the vulnerability is present and not fixed.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 90613366ab07018248f7c2974367bcb6afd37499
Author: Julien Lancelot <julien.lancelot@sonarsource.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 20 11:06:01 2017 +0200

    SONAR-9128 Use protobuf in api/users/search

diff --git a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java b/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
index 4fd0d0da761..7e42599308b 100644
--- a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
+++ b/server/so
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'towsUser' function sets the 'externalIdentity' field (via 'FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY') inside the 'if (userSession.isLoggedIn())' block rather than the 'if (userSession.isSystemAdministrator())' block, exposing it to all authenticated users.

bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 90613366ab07018248f7c2974367bcb6afd37499
Author: Julien Lancelot <julien.lancelot@sonarsource.com>
Date:   Thu Apr 20 11:06:01 2017 +0200

    SONAR-9128 Use protobuf in api/users/search

diff --git a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java b/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
index 4fd0d0da761..7e42599308b 100644
--- a/server/sonar-server/src/main/java/org/sonar/server/user/ws/SearchAction.java
+++ b/server/so
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'towsUser' method adds the 'FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY' to the response using 'setIfNeeded(FIELD_EXTERNAL_IDENTITY, fields, user.getExternalIdentity(), userBuilder::setExternalIdentity)' under the condition 'if (userSession.isLoggedIn())'. It does not check for system administrator privileges ('userSession.isSystemAdministrator()'), which exposes the externalIdentity to any authenticated user.
